ingredients.item: ½ cup (25 g) packed roughly chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.original: ½ cup (25 g) packed roughly chopped fresh parsley

ingredients.item: 4 tablespoons olive oil, divided, plus more for greasing
ingredients.original: 4 tablespoons olive oil, divided, plus more for greasing

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon seven spice (or substitute with ground allspice)
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon seven spice (or substitute with ground allspice)

ingredients.item: 4 small, thick Greek-style round pitas (4 inches, or 10 cm), cut in half (see Note)
ingredients.original: 4 small, thick Greek-style round pitas (4 inches, or 10 cm), cut in half (see Note)

ingredients.item: 1 Add the onion, garlic, and parsley to a small food processor and pulse until chopped very fine. (If you do not have a food processor, you can chop it very finely with a knife.) Using your hands, squeeze out and discard as much moisture as you can from this vegetable mixture and transfer it to a large bowl.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the onion, garlic, and parsley to a small food processor and pulse until chopped very fine. (If you do not have a food processor, you can chop it very finely with a knife.) Using your hands, squeeze out and discard as much moisture as you can from this vegetable mixture and transfer it to a large bowl.

ingredients.item: 2 Add the ground beef, 2 tablespoons of the oil, the pepper, salt, seven spice, and pomegranate molasses. Mix well using your hands, ensuring all the spices are well incorporated.
ingredients.original: 2 Add the ground beef, 2 tablespoons of the oil, the pepper, salt, seven spice, and pomegranate molasses. Mix well using your hands, ensuring all the spices are well incorporated.

ingredients.item: 3 Open the pita halves to form pockets. Stuff each one with about 2 tablespoons of the meat mixture, ensuring the meat layer is roughly ¼ to ½ inch (6 to 13 mm) thick. Press down to flatten and ensure the filling comes all the way to the edge.
ingredients.original: 3 Open the pita halves to form pockets. Stuff each one with about 2 tablespoons of the meat mixture, ensuring the meat layer is roughly ¼ to ½ inch (6 to 13 mm) thick. Press down to flatten and ensure the filling comes all the way to the edge.

ingredients.item: 4 Brush the outsides of the pitas with the remaining 2 or 3 tablespoons oil on all sides.
ingredients.original: 4 Brush the outsides of the pitas with the remaining 2 or 3 tablespoons oil on all sides.

ingredients.item: 5 Preheat a medium or large cast-iron grill pan over high heat. Brush with olive oil, then place 2 or 3 pitas (however many you can fit) in the pan and sear for 2 minutes per side, pressing down lightly with a spatula. Sear the edges as well, especially the exposed meat side. Continue to cook, flipping the arayes every 1 to 2 minutes, until the beef is cooked through and the outside is golden, 5 to 7 minutes total. Repeat with the remaining pitas.
ingredients.original: 5 Preheat a medium or large cast-iron grill pan over high heat. Brush with olive oil, then place 2 or 3 pitas (however many you can fit) in the pan and sear for 2 minutes per side, pressing down lightly with a spatula. Sear the edges as well, especially the exposed meat side. Continue to cook, flipping the arayes every 1 to 2 minutes, until the beef is cooked through and the outside is golden, 5 to 7 minutes total. Repeat with the remaining pitas.

ingredients.item: 6 Serve immediately with tarator, toum, and/or ketchup for dipping and french fries.
ingredients.original: 6 Serve immediately with tarator, toum, and/or ketchup for dipping and french fries.
